why: THE REVIEWER'S CASE, AND THE FALSE GREEN THE ROLLUP COVERAGE RULE COULD NOT CLOSE. It is `rollup-expected-status.json` with the EXPECTED StatusContext DELETED FROM THE ROLLUP — a rollup that is merely SHORT, which nothing can detect (it carries no total and cannot be proven complete). The required status `ci/jenkins/pr-merge` has NEVER BEEN POSTED, so REST cannot report it either (there is no EXPECTED state in the commit-status API). Every rule that reads the artifact quantifies over the rows that ARE there: the check run passes, both markers are present, containment holds, the coverage rule has NOTHING TO CHECK — and this used to be GREEN, on a PR blocked on a check nobody has run. What refuses is the one source that does not depend on anything showing up: the BASE BRANCH DECLARED that check REQUIRED, and it is not present and passing. Weaken `required-set-is-passed` and this goes GREEN — which is exactly the kill that matters
